author: Sarah Mitchell
text: Absolutely breathtaking experience! The hike to Gergeti Trinity Church exceeded all expectations. Our guide Giorgi was incredibly knowledgeable about the history and made the steep sections manageable. The views of Kazbek with the church in the foreground — just wow. A must-do when visiting Georgia!

author: Thomas Weber
text: The Mestia to Ushguli trek was the highlight of our three weeks in the Caucasus. Four days of stunning mountain scenery, genuine Svan hospitality, and some of the best food we had in Georgia. The guesthouses were simple but charming, and our guide Dato handled every river crossing and route decision with expert ease.

author: Emma Johansson
text: Beautiful hike through Juta Valley! The Chaukhi rock formations are spectacular and unlike anything I've seen in Scandinavia. The wildflower meadows were stunning in July. Only giving 4 stars because the weather turned and we couldn't make it all the way to the pass, but the team handled the situation safely and professionally.

author: James Chen
text: What a fantastic introduction to Tbilisi! Nino's knowledge of Georgian history and culture is encyclopedic, and her passion is contagious. The wine tasting was a wonderful surprise, and the lunch at the traditional restaurant was the best meal of our trip. Highly recommend starting your Georgia visit with this tour.

author: Maria Rodriguez
text: Tusheti is pure magic. Five days of complete wilderness immersion — no phone signal, no crowds, just mountains, meadows, and medieval villages. We saw tur, eagles, and even fresh bear tracks! The camping was comfortable and the food was amazing considering we were in the middle of nowhere. This trek changed my perspective on what adventure travel can be.

author: Yuki Tanaka
text: Shatili is like stepping into another world. The fortress village is hauntingly beautiful and the Khevsur history is fascinating. The drive through the mountains to get there is an adventure in itself! Sleeping in the restored tower was a unique experience. The Anatori crypts hike on day two was eerie but memorable. A culturally rich experience I won't forget.
